pageTimings.text:
Wren and Flint were going to the Whispering Meadow.
Flint had a map.
Wren had a direction spell.
"We will be perfectly fine," said Flint.

Flint's map said go left.
Wren's spell said go right.
"Straight ahead," they agreed.
This turned out to be wrong.

An hour later, nothing looked right.
The trees were wrong.
The paths had gone.
"Where are we?" asked Wren.
Flint checked his map.

"We are here," said Flint.
He pointed at the map.
Wren looked around.
Grey fog was appearing between the trees.
"That is the Fog Marsh," said Wren.
"We are not near the meadow."

Wren tried her direction spell.
The arrow spun three times.
Then it pointed at Flint.
"That's you," said Wren.
"I know I'm here," said Flint.

Flint opened his satchel.
Seven maps fell out.
One was of the coast.
One was upside down.
None of them were quite right.

They sat down under a large mushroom.
They were tired.
They were lost.
The mushroom glowed cheerfully.
Wren looked at it.

"I wonder," said Wren.
She closed her spell book.
She spoke to the mushroom.
Very quietly.
She asked it the way.

The mushroom's glow changed.
It pulsed once — pointing.
Another mushroom far away pulsed the same way.
And another.
A trail of glowing mushrooms lit up through the trees.

Wren and Flint followed the mushroom trail.
The forest opened up.
The golden light of the Whispering Meadow was ahead.
"Oh," said Flint.

They arrived at the Whispering Meadow.
It was exactly where they had been going.
Flint wrote something on his map.
"Mushroom trail," he said.
"I should have had that already."

"Why didn't we try that first?" asked Flint.
"I don't know," said Wren.
She looked back at the mushroom trail.
"They seemed happy about it though."
The last mushroom blinked once and went out.
